flags.concern: Timeline canvas shot-group overlay: plan places the clickable badge inside a `pointer-events-none` border overlay, so the badge hit target would not fire.
flags.evidence: Revised plan to address all 9 flags: (1) Callbacks now pass full group identity {shotId, clipIds, rowId} instead of just shotId. (2) Asset registration bypasses registerGenerationAsset entirely — directly creates AssetRegistryEntry with type:'video/mp4' and calls patchRegistry+registerAsset (matching useStaleVariants pattern). (3) No video duration fetch needed — clip uses shot group time span with from:0/to:duration. (4) Extends existing shotGroupMenu state with clipIds, rowId, hasFinalVideo instead of creating a parallel menu. (5) Context menu pattern confirmed (not panel). (6) 2+ clip threshold confirmed. (7) Unit tests dropped per user note — TypeScript compilation sufficient. (8) Removed the separate useFinalVideoAvailable unit test from success criteria.

flags.concern: Final-video asset registration: `registerGenerationAsset()` ignores the plan's top-level `mimeType` sketch, so signed final-video URLs can be classified as images unless `variantType` or `metadata.content_type` is supplied.

flags.concern: Final-video duration handling: `useShotFinalVideos()` does not provide duration and the timeline uses `clip.to - clip.from` as truth, so the proposed in-place trim can overrun shorter source media.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts` against the proposed `useFinalVideoAvailable` state model: stale reminders are dismissed per `assetKey` and can clear when the asset changes, but the plan dismisses by `shotId`, which would hide any newer final video for the same shot for the rest of the session even though `useShotFinalVideos()` exposes a changing final-video `id`.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts`: `flushGroup()` only emits groups with at least two clip ids and shots with fewer than two images are skipped up front, so the plan's assumption matches current code but leaves the explicit single-clip-shot question unresolved rather than answered by the repository.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`. The repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endTime - startTime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.
flags.evidence: Checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`. The repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endTime - startTime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.

flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216`. The revised `TimelineEditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `clipOrder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.
flags.evidence: Checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216`. The revised `TimelineEditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `clipOrder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked shot-group action plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. The canvas already has one shot-group menu state and portal renderer for group actions, so introducing a second parallel menu state for final-video actions would duplicate the same concern in the same component; extending the existing menu model with richer group payload looks more aligned with the current structure.

flags.concern: Final-video reminder state: dismissing by `shotId` instead of final-video id still suppresses later final outputs for the same shot during the same session.
flags.evidence: The revised plan keeps `dismissedShotIds: Set<string>` in `useFinalVideoAvailable`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` surfaces per-shot final-video records keyed by a changing generation `id` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`.

flags.concern: Timeline media duration: the revised plan still sizes the final-video clip to the shot-group span without any duration source, so shorter final videos can still overrun their real media length.
flags.evidence: `useShotFinalVideos()` fetches no duration in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:23-27`, while timeline duration is derived from `clip.to - clip.from` in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35` and passed directly to the Remotion `Video` trim props in `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx:114-123`.

flags.concern: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked related asset-insertion code in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:150-271` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:24-33`. The broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `clipOrder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/UI change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useStaleVariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:39-60`. The plan still dismisses by `shotId`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useShotFinalVideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.
